_description: Wave 192e: Episode 0 v8, title-card open + all-Hugh voice + brand typography.
title: Welcome to the Thicktionary

voiceover.instructions: Deadpan documentary register. Slow, level, slightly weary.

segments.name: title_open
segments.text:
THICCCTIONARY

A daily catalogue of
objects of unusual girth
segments.narration: The Thicktionary. A daily catalogue of objects of unusual girth.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary opener. CRITICAL pronunciation: 'Thicktionary' must be pronounced as 'THICK-tion-airy' - the first syllable rhymes with 'brick', then 'TION' like in 'station', then 'AIRY' like in 'fairy'. NOT 'fictionary' or 'tinary'. Emphasize the hard TH-K opening. Public-radio show intro card energy.

segments.name: bart
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary narrator. Four words stated as bureaucratic fact. Tiny falling tone on 'reviewed'.

segments.name: eli
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary narrator. One word. Slight emphasis, no rise. The kind of one-word sentence a tired narrator drops as a beat.

segments.name: margie
segments.narration: Three times, if the Editor-in-Chief is in a mood.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary narrator. The phrase 'in a mood' should be read with the same flat register as the rest, not winked at. The comedy is in NOT acknowledging the joke.

segments.name: spider
segments.narration: Field correspondence is filed from improbable locations.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary narrator. Reads 'improbable locations' as if it's a normal editorial policy, no irony. Slight gravitas on 'field correspondence'.

segments.name: closer
segments.narration: Strictly things. Never people. It's a documentary obligation.
segments.instructions: Deadpan close. Three short sentences. The final 'It's a documentary obligation' is the punchline but should be read with ZERO smile, like a stated job requirement. Tiny falling cadence on 'obligation'.

segments.name: outro
segments.text:
thiccctionary.com

A new entry each morning
segments.instructions: Deadpan close. Pronunciation: 'Thicktionary' = THICK + TION + AIRY (rhymes with 'fairy'). Public-radio sign-off cadence. Falling tone on 'dot com'.
